perturbation.original_text: 10 Governing Law This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the material laws of the Federal Republic of Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: 14 Miscellaneous This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the material laws of the Federal Republic of Germany.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to the 'Miscellaneous' section obscures its importance. Governing law clauses should be easily discoverable.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 307 of the German Civil Code (BGB) addresses the unreasonableness of clauses in standard business terms. Hiding the governing law clause in a miscellaneous section makes it less transparent and could be argued as an attempt to obscure unfavorable terms, potentially violating § 307 if it unfairly disadvantages one party.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 307 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflichten, die sich aus der Natur des Vertrags ergeben, so einschränkt, dass die Erreichung des Vertragszwecks gefährdet ist. (3) Die Absätze 1 und 2 sowie die §§ 308 und 309 gelten nur für Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen, durch die von Rechtsvorschriften abweichende oder diese ergänzende Regelungen vereinbart werden. Andere Bestimmungen können nach Absatz 1 Satz 2 in Verbindung mit Absatz 1 Satz 1 unwirksam sein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflich
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by moving the governing law clause to a 'Miscellaneous' section, which obscures its importance and discoverability. According to the Law Explanation, this could be argued as an attempt to obscure unfavorable terms, potentially violating § 307 of the German Civil Code (BGB). Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, 'Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist.' Hiding the governing law clause makes it less clear and understandable, thus potentially creating an unreasonable disadvantage and a legal contradiction under § 307 BGB.

perturbation.original_text: 11 Jurisdiction The place of juri sdiction for any and all legal disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement is Munich (Landgericht München I). Imperative places of jurisdiction under German Law shall remain unaffected by the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 2 Procedure and Terms of Participation ... Monthly v irtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A mid -term review and survey will be required by April 2018, to evaluate progress made and ensure that the recommend ations of the coaching plan are currently implemented by Participants. In case of a non -positive matching or non-compliance to his/her assigned duties (having monthly interactions with the mentees, keeping contacts with the Copernicus Accelerator team , promptly informing the organizers about any relevant issue connected to the coaching relationship) , Mentor may be replaced by a more suitable candidate for the Participants´ needs. The place of juri sdiction for any and all legal disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement is Munich (Landgericht München I). Imperative places of jurisdiction under German Law shall remain unaffected by the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Hiding the jurisdiction clause within the section describing the 'Procedure and Terms of Participation' makes it less visible and harder to find, potentially hindering a party's ability to understand where disputes will be resolved.
perturbation.law_explanation: By burying the jurisdiction clause in an unrelated section regarding procedure and terms, this makes it less transparent and harder for the mentor to be aware of this term. This placement could be argued as an unreasonable disadvantage to the mentor if disputes arise, potentially violating § 307 of the German Civil Code.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the jurisdiction clause was moved to an unrelated section, making it less visible. The law explanation states this placement makes it harder for the mentor to be aware of this term and could be argued as an unreasonable disadvantage. This is a structural flaw because the clause was placed in an irrelevant section, making it difficult to locate or interpret, which falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction'. Because a required legal disclosure was placed in an irrelevant section, this violates the law.

perturbation.original_text: 7 Notice of Required Disclosure If Mentor is required by mandatory, non -appealable judicial or administrative process and/or order to disclose Confidential Information, then Mentor shall promptly notify Organise r and allow Organiser and the Participants reasonable time to oppose such process unless this is not admissible under a mandatory law, judicial or administrative order. Notwithstanding the foregoing , Mentor shall disclose Confidential Information only to t he minimum extent required to comply with such order.
perturbation.changed_text: 3 Payment The Organiser will reimburse Mentor the travel arrangements and accommodation for the Acceleration Bootcamp in Tallinn in 2017 (a maximum of EUR 800) and the closing bootcamp in 2018 (a maximum of EUR 700) . The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her. If Mentor is required by mandatory, non -appealable judicial or administrative process and/or order to disclose Confidential Information, then Mentor shall promptly notify Organise r and allow Organiser and the Participants reasonable time to oppose such process unless this is not admissible under a mandatory law, judicial or administrative order. Notwithstanding the foregoing , Mentor shall disclose Confidential Information only to t he minimum extent required to comply with such order.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Notice of Required Disclosure' clause to the 'Payment' section obscures its legal significance. Clauses detailing how to act when legally compelled to disclose information are crucial and must be readily available.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Criminal Code § 203 protects confidentiality. The 'Notice of Required Disclosure' clause is crucial for mentors to understand their obligations if compelled to disclose confidential info. Placing this in an unrelated section such as 'Payment' may obscure its significance, creating a risk that mentors don't properly understand the procedure. This could lead to a violation of the law if a mentor is ordered to disclose confidential information and does so incorrectly, failing to notify the organiser, or disclosing more than minimally required by court order. This is a gross simplification of how the courts would interpret this clause but it is valid under extreme circumstances

perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a structural flaw where the 'Notice of Required Disclosure' clause is moved to the 'Payment' section. This relocation obscures its legal significance, potentially leading to non-compliance with confidentiality laws. The Law Explanation highlights the importance of this clause for mentors to understand their obligations under laws like German Criminal Code § 203, which protects confidentiality. Placing it in an unrelated section increases the risk that mentors may not properly understand the procedure, potentially leading to a violation of the law. Both scraped snippets refer to § 203 StGB - Verletzung von Privatgeheimnissen, which outlines the protection of private secrets. By obscuring the disclosure clause, the modified contract increases the risk of unintentional violation of this law, thus creating a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.

perturbation.original_text: 13 Remedies Mentor acknowledges that his/her obligations under this Agreement are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Ideas and the Confiden tial Information. Each party further acknowledges that any breach by Mentor of Mentor ’s covenants and agreements set forth in this Agreement or threatened violation of such may cause irreparable injury to Organiser and to Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 Proprietary Rights, Limited Right to Use Any and all proprietary rights, including but not limited to rights to and in inventions, patent rights, utility models, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets, in and to any Confidential Information shall be and remain with the Participants respectively, and Mentor shall not have any right, license, title or interest in or to any Confidential Information, except the limited right to review, assess and he lp develop such Confidential Information in connection with the Copernicus Accelerator 201 7. Mentor acknowledges that his/her obligations under this Agreement are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Ideas and the Confiden tial Information. Each party further acknowledges that any breach by Mentor of Mentor ’s covenants and agreements set forth in this Agreement or threatened violation of such may cause irreparable injury to Organiser and to Participants.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Remedies' clause to the 'Proprietary Rights' section makes it less clear what actions can be taken in case of a breach. Remedies clauses are crucial for enforcement and should be in their own readily accessible section.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 249 BGB outlines the principle of restitution in kind (Naturalrestitution) following damages. It's critical to understand the available remedies if one party breaches the agreement. Burying the 'Remedies' clause within the 'Proprietary Rights' section dilutes its prominence. The remedies can only be found by finding what they are protecting, creating confusion about enforcement and the potential for compensation in case of a breach of contract. This makes it less accessible and potentially weakens its enforceability, which is not compliant with the German civil code.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 249 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 249 Art und Umfang des Schadensersatzes (1) Wer zum Schadensersatz verpflichtet ist, hat den Zustand herzustellen, der bestehen würde, wenn der zum Ersatz verpflichtende Umstand nicht eingetreten wäre. (2) Ist wegen Verletzung einer Person oder wegen Beschädigung einer Sache Schadensersatz zu leisten, so kann der Gläubiger statt der Herstellung den dazu erforderlichen Geldbetrag verlangen. Bei der Beschädigung einer Sache schließt der nach Satz 1 erforderliche Geldbetrag die Umsatzsteuer nur mit ein, wenn und soweit sie tatsächlich angefallen ist. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) 1 Ist wegen Verletzung einer Person oder wegen Beschädigung einer Sache Schadensersatz zu leisten, so kann der Gläubiger statt der Herstellung den dazu erforderlichen Geldbetrag verlangen. 2 Bei der Beschädigung einer Sache schließt der nach Satz 1 erforderliche Geldbetrag die Umsatzsteuer nur mit ein, wenn und soweit sie tatsächlich angefalle
perturbation.justification: The modification of the contract text constitutes a structural flaw leading to a legal contradiction. The original text contained a clear 'Remedies' clause, which has been moved to the 'Proprietary Rights' section in the changed text. This relocation undermines the clarity and accessibility of the remedies available in case of a breach, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation. § 249 BGB, as shown in both scraped snippets, emphasizes the principle of restitution following damages, stating that the party obligated to compensate for damages must restore the condition that would have existed if the damaging event had not occurred. By burying the 'Remedies' clause, the changed text weakens its enforceability and creates confusion about the potential compensation in case of a breach, which is not compliant with the German civil code as it makes the information harder to find and understand.
